sourceRows.FindingKind: Nielsen identities of the Standard Model and mass definition
sourceRows.Summary: Shows that Nielsen identities control gauge-parameter dependence and that complex-pole masses of physical Standard Model fields are gauge independent to all orders, including mixing.
sourceRows.PredictionImpact: Defines a physical pole-mass boundary; does not derive GU W/Z/H source rows.

sourceRows.FindingKind: Widths and partial widths from Nielsen identities
sourceRows.Summary: Uses Nielsen identities to prove gauge independence of pole residues and associated partial-width definitions to all orders.
sourceRows.PredictionImpact: Useful for resonance extraction consistency; not a mass-source law.

sourceRows.FindingKind: ArXiv record for Nielsen-identity width analysis
sourceRows.Summary: Open source record for the unstable-particle Nielsen identity analysis and Z-gamma sector discussion.
sourceRows.PredictionImpact: Reference access for the same gauge-independence boundary.

sourceRows.Summary: Reviews how complex-pole renormalization supports gauge-invariant treatment of unstable particles in perturbative QFT.
sourceRows.PredictionImpact: Mass-convention and finite-width boundary; still imports Lagrangian inputs.

sourceRows.FindingKind: Gauge-invariant resonant amplitudes via pinch technique
sourceRows.Summary: Builds gauge-independent resonant transition amplitudes for Standard Model processes involving W/Z interactions.
sourceRows.PredictionImpact: Amplitude extraction lead; no GU-local W/Z/H source scale.

sourceRows.Summary: Reviews Slavnov-Taylor and Ward-Takahashi identity control for Standard Model perturbative calculations.
sourceRows.PredictionImpact: BRST/identity control template; no GU pole equation or mass values.

decision: Do not promote W/Z or Higgs physical masses from Nielsen identities, complex-pole gauge independence, the complex-mass scheme, or pinch-technique resonance extraction. These sources define a rigorous gauge-independence boundary for physical pole masses and amplitudes, but the current repository still lacks GU-local BRST/Slavnov-Taylor control, observed W/Z/H operators, pole equations, target-independent scale/coupling lineage, Higgs scalar-source lineage, and GeV normalization.
nextRequiredArtifact: A GU-local BRST/Slavnov-Taylor or equivalent identity proving gauge-parameter control for the observed sector.
Gauge-invariant photon/W/Z/H two-point functions or correlation matrices with pole and residue extraction.
Target-independent W/Z scale, weak-mixing, gauge-coupling, scalar-source, and GeV-unit lineage feeding those pole equations.
A proof that the extracted GU poles correspond to observed physical W, Z, and Higgs states before target comparison.
